name: Retool
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Retool's design language — an internal-tool builder brand built on a warm cream canvas (#fbf6ea), GT Sectra serif at every display tier, and a signature signal-orange (#ff5d00) voltage. The brand reads as 'serious software for serious operators' — magazine-cover headlines, dashboard mockups as hero, generous use of real product screenshots.

design_language.motion_signature: 200ms ease-out on hover; signature is the dashboard-mockup scroll-reveal — built-internal-tool screenshots fade in at 500ms with a 12px translate; orange CTAs have a subtle 80ms press-down.

trademark_signals: GT Sectra serif at every display tier — magazine-cover scale headlines (72–96px) at weight 500 with -0.02em tracking — refuses the dev-tool sans-everywhere default
Warm cream canvas (#fbf6ea) — deliberately not white, deliberately not gray — pairs with serif display to create an editorial feel for a developer product
Signal-orange voltage (#ff5d00) appears only on CTAs and as the underline accent on selected display words — never a hero gradient, never a background
Dashboard-mockup-as-hero — every solutions page leads with a real Retool-built internal tool screenshot (admin panel, ops dashboard, approval queue) — the brand shows the product chrome
Customer logo wall right below hero — DoorDash, Brex, Mercury, Plaid, Marqeta — operator-brand-name credibility is the brand's second-most-visible move after the hero
ai_slop_avoided: Don't substitute GT Sectra with Inter or Söhne for display. The serif at hero scale IS the brand voice
Don't paint the canvas pure white. The cream tint is the brand's editorial differentiator from every other dev tool
Don't put orange on a hero gradient or as a background. The orange is scarce — CTAs and selected underlines only
Don't replace the dashboard-mockup hero with an abstract illustration. Real internal-tool screenshots are the brand's trust signal
Don't bold the serif beyond weight 500. The brand's elegance is in the typeface, not the weight
